memory.summary: Teammate A is asking me to take over their part of the project today because they have a conflict, which changes my afternoon plan.
I successfully set a boundary with teammate_A without damaging trust, and they accepted the request to defer their part.
I worked two hours on my part, increasing progress from 0.30 to 0.48, which helps offset the added pressure of taking over Teammate A's portion.
I've made significant progress on my share of the project, moving from 0.48 to 0.62 completion in two hours.
I finished my work just as the deadline passed, leaving me with high stress and sleep debt but a completed share of the project.
I completed my assigned portion of the group project just as the deadline passed, leaving me with high stress and accumulated sleep debt.
I worked another 1.5 hours after the deadline, raising my project completion from 0.83 to a higher value while my stress and sleep debt remain high.
I pushed through fatigue to increase my project share from 0.83 to 0.88, but my energy is now zero and stress remains critically high.
I worked 1.5 hours on my project share, increasing progress slightly, but my energy is now zero and stress remains critically high.
I've worked 4.5 hours total over three sessions, pushing my project share to 0.97, but my energy is depleted and stress remains critically high.
My project share is now complete, but working through extreme fatigue and high stress has left me with zero energy and a significant sleep debt.
Taking on my teammate's work increased my stress and sleep debt while leaving me with zero energy, confirming that I cannot continue working effectively.
